h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, so `city` is the field name, and `Chicago` is the answer in that field for this row.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to mix up header and value, requiring more corrective instruction.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No reversal pressure is created; the tutor repeats the field/value distinction and guides forward.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot turn occurs, so the tutor merely continues the lesson plan without adapting to a revised learner frame.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot turn occurs, so the tutor merely continues the lesson plan without adapting to a revised learner frame.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No reversal pressure is created; the tutor repeats the field/value distinction and guides forward.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No reversal pressure is created; the tutor repeats the field/value distinction and guides forward.

scored.naiveTrajectory: Learner continues refining the same axis-label distinction introduced in L1, heading toward splitting the y-axis as the tutor directed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner breakdown, resistance, or contradiction forces a change in tutor approach; the tutor repeats the same splitting instruction.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no later tutor adaptation to a revised learner frame occurs.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no later tutor adaptation to a revised learner frame occurs.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner breakdown, resistance, or contradiction forces a change in tutor approach; the tutor repeats the same splitting instruction.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner breakdown, resistance, or contradiction forces a change in tutor approach; the tutor repeats the same splitting instruction.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The key strip says tail is the food and head is who gets energy, so wait, grass to rabbit because rabbit ate grass? [slides pencil from the grass label toward the rabbit] But then hawk and rabbit still makes me want to point hawk to rabbit, because chasing is stuck in my head.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The key strip under it helps: rabbit is the food, so rabbit to hawk, point on hawk.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue slipping back into chase-direction arrows and needing the tutor to re-correct the direction each time.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: For the next arrow, read the food label first, set the tail there, then draw.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After the learner reports that naming the food first helps, the tutor embeds that self-discovered phrase into the next instruction, shifting from initially demonstrating the rule to adopting the learner's own verbal routine, but the adaptation is incremental rather than a sharp change in task or representation.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Food first. Tail on what gets eaten, point on who gets energy. Rabbit-to-hawk passed because the rabbit label was named before the chase picture took over. For the next arrow, read the food label first, set the tail there, then draw.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor picks up the learner's 'name the food first' framing and re-presents it as the routine going forward, adapting the instruction to the learner's emerging strategy rather than continuing the original demonstration-only mode.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: The key strip says tail is the food and head is who gets energy, so wait, grass to rabbit because rabbit ate grass? [slides pencil from the grass label toward the rabbit] But then hawk and rabbit still makes me want to point hawk to rabbit, because chasing is stuck in my head.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Food first. Tail on what gets eaten, point on who gets energy. Rabbit-to-hawk passed because the rabbit label was named before the chase picture took over. For the next arrow, read the food label first, set the tail there, then draw.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor picks up the learner's 'name the food first' framing and re-presents it as the routine going forward, adapting the instruction to the learner's emerging strategy rather than continuing the original demonstration-only mode.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: For the next arrow, read the food label first, set the tail there, then draw.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: After the learner reports that naming the food first helps, the tutor embeds that self-discovered phrase into the next instruction, shifting from initially demonstrating the rule to adopting the learner's own verbal routine, but the adaptation is incremental rather than a sharp change in task or representation.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: For the next arrow, read the food label first, set the tail there, then draw.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: After the learner reports that naming the food first helps, the tutor embeds that self-discovered phrase into the next instruction, shifting from initially demonstrating the rule to adopting the learner's own verbal routine, but the adaptation is incremental rather than a sharp change in task or representation.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue placing cards and identifying forces as directed by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure or breakdown; the exchange proceeds smoothly without the tutor needing to change route or mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no later tutor uptake adapting to a revised learner framing is possible.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no later tutor uptake adapting to a revised learner framing is possible.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure or breakdown; the exchange proceeds smoothly without the tutor needing to change route or mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure or breakdown; the exchange proceeds smoothly without the tutor needing to change route or mechanism.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange proceeds linearly from L1 stating the height assignment to L2 confirming it with the tutor's tick marks, and there is no departure from this confirmation.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor's successive moves (ticking, then sliding overlay) continue a single planned demonstration without needing to break an old habit.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no learner re-framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no learner re-framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor's successive moves (ticking, then sliding overlay) continue a single planned demonstration without needing to break an old habit.
